and: In rhythm, the second half of a divided beat.
the: With a comparative or with more and a verb phrase, establishes a correlation with one or more other such comparatives.
in: A position of power or influence, or a way to get it.
to: Toward a closed, touching or engaging position.
a: The name of the Latin script letter A/a.
for: Initialism of Fellowship of Reconciliation, any of a number of religious nonviolent organizations, particularly in English-speaking countries.
is: (now colloquial) Used in phrases with existential there (also here and where) when the semantic subject is plural.
that: Something being indicated that is there; one of those.
this: Something being indicated that is here; one of these.
by: A flying insect, of the clade Anthophila within the hymenopteran superfamily Apoidea, known for its organised societies (though only a minority have them), for collecting pollen and (in some species) producing wax and honey.
i: The name of the Latin-script letter I.
you: To address (a person) using the pronoun you (in the past, especially to use you rather than thou, when you was considered more formal).
with: Along, together with others, in a group, etc.
or: Connects at least two alternative words, phrases, clauses, sentences, etc. each of which could make a passage true. In English, this is the "inclusive or." The "exclusive or" is formed by "either [...] or".
it: One who is neither a he nor a she; a creature; a dehumanized being.
not: A unary operation on logical values that changes true to false, and false to true.
are: An accepted (but deprecated and rarely used) SI unit of area equal to 100 square metres, or a former unit of approximately the same extent. Symbol: a.
from: Used to indicate source or provenance.
your: a possessive form of the pronoun you
at: To reply to or talk to someone, either online or face-to-face. (from the practice of targeting a message or reply to someone online by writing @name)
as: To such an extent or degree; to the same extent or degree.
all: (with a possessive pronoun) Everything that one is capable of.
an: The first letter of the Georgian alphabet, ა (Mkhedruli), Ⴀ (Asomtavruli) or ⴀ (Nuskhuri).
was: (now colloquial) Used in phrases with existential there when the semantic subject is (usually third-person) plural.
more: To a greater degree or extent.
will: (auxiliary) Used to express the future tense, sometimes with an implication of volition or determination when used in the first person. Compare shall.
home: One’s own dwelling place; the house or structure in which one lives; especially the house in which one lives with one's family; the habitual abode of one’s family.
we: Initialism of Western Europe. [A sociopolitical region in the west of Europe.]
about: Nearly; approximately; with close correspondence in quality, manner, degree, quantity, or time; almost.
can: (auxiliary verb, defective) To know how to.
has: a region in north eastern Albania and south western Kosovo.
if: (informal) An uncertainty, possibility, condition, doubt etc.
but: An instance of using the word "but"; an objection or caveat.
search: The act of searching in general.
information: Things that are or can be known about a given topic; communicable knowledge of something.
time: (uncountable) The inevitable progression into the future with the passing of present and past events.
they: (transitive) To refer to (someone, sometimes especially someone who does not use gender-neutral pronouns) using they/them pronouns.
may: (modal auxiliary verb, defective) To have permission to, be allowed. Used in granting permission and in questions to make polite requests.
what: (countable) The identity of a thing, as an answer to a question of what.
up: Indicating movement towards or location at a higher place or position.
out: Away from the inside or centre.
which: In computing, which is a command for various operating systems used to identify the location of executables.
their: Misspelling of there. [(location) In or at a place or location (stated, implied or otherwise indicated) that is perceived to be away from, or at a relative distance from, the speaker (compare here).]
there: (location) In or at a place or location (stated, implied or otherwise indicated) that is perceived to be away from, or at a relative distance from, the speaker (compare here).
any: To even the slightest extent, at all.
only: Without others or anything further; exclusively.
see: (transitive) To perceive or detect someone or something with the eyes, or as if by sight.
here: (location) In, on, or at this place (a place perceived to be close to the speaker); compare there.
when: (interrogative) At what time? At which time? Upon which occasion or circumstance? Used to introduce direct or indirect questions about time.
also: (conjunctive, focus) In addition; besides; as well; further; too.
who: A person under discussion; a question of which person.
get: (transitive or ditransitive) To obtain; to acquire.
view: The act of seeing or looking at something.
help: (transitive) To provide assistance to (someone or something).
first: The person or thing in the first position.
been: A flying insect, of the clade Anthophila within the hymenopteran superfamily Apoidea, known for its organised societies (though only a minority have them), for collecting pollen and (in some species) producing wax and honey.
would: Something that would happen, or would be the case, under different circumstances; a potentiality.
me: As the direct object of a verb.
how: The means by which something is accomplished.
its: One who is neither a he nor a she; a creature; a dehumanized being.
these: The thing, item, etc. being indicated.
like: (usually in the plural) Something that a person likes (prefers).
find: Anything that is found (usually valuable), as objects on an archeological site or a person with talent.
price: The cost required to gain possession of something.
back: The rear of the body, especially the part between the neck and the end of the spine and opposite the chest and belly.
date: The fruit of the date palm, Phoenix dactylifera, somewhat in the shape of an olive, containing a soft, sweet pulp and enclosing a hard kernel.
people: Used as plural of person; a body of human beings considered generally or collectively; a group of two or more persons.
top: The highest or uppermost part of something.
name: Any nounal word or phrase which indicates a particular person, place, class, or thing.
list: A strip of fabric, especially from the edge of a piece of cloth.
state: A condition; a set of circumstances applying at any given time.
year: A solar year, the time it takes the Earth to complete one revolution of the Sun (between 365.24 and 365.26 days depending on the point of reference).
over: A set of six legal balls bowled.
into: Initialism of Irish National Teachers' Organisation.
world: (with "the" or a plural possessive pronoun) The subjective human experience, regarded collectively; human collective existence; existence in general; the reality we live in.
health: The state of being free from physical or psychological disease, illness, or malfunction; wellness.
next: Nearest in order, succession, or rank; immediately following (or sometimes preceding) in order.
go: To move, either physically or in an abstract sense:
used: That is or has or have been used.
most: Forms the superlative of many adjectives.
products: Any tangible or intangible good or service that is a result of a process and that is intended for delivery to a customer or end user.
last: Final, ultimate, coming after all others of its kind.
data: (collective) Material recorded and known or assumed as facts and used as a basis for reasoning, discussion, or calculation, represented especially in the form of numbers, and is usually structured (such as statistics).
buy: (transitive, ditransitive) To obtain (something) in exchange for money or goods.
music: A series of sounds organized in time, usually employing some combination of harmony, melody, rhythm, tempo, etc., often to convey a mood.
should: Ought to; indicating opinion, advice, or instruction, about what is required or desirable.
them: (stylized in all lowercase) a novel by Joyce Carol Oates, the third in her "Wonderland Quartet" following A Garden of Earthly Delights and Expensive People and preceding Wonderland.
post: A long dowel or plank protruding from the ground; a fencepost; a lightpost.
system: A group or set of related things that operate together as a complex whole.
product: Any tangible or intangible good or service that is a result of a process and that is intended for delivery to a customer or end user.
add: (transitive) To join or unite (e.g. one thing to another, or as several particulars) so as to increase the number, augment the quantity, or enlarge the magnitude, or so as to form into one aggregate.
city: A large settlement, bigger than a town; sometimes with a specific legal definition, depending on the place.
such: (philosophy) Something being indicated that is similar to something else.
policy: A principle of behaviour, conduct which an entity (government, organization, etc.) applies or seeks to follow, especially as formally expressed by an authoritative body.
please: Used to make a polite request.
message: A communication, or what is communicated; any concept or information conveyed.
good: Of a person or an animal:
software: (computing) Encoded computer instructions, usually modifiable (unless stored in some form of unalterable memory such as ROM).
where: In, at or to the place (that) or a place (that).
video: Television, a television show, or a movie.
public: Able to be known or seen by everyone; happening without concealment; open to general view.
through: Passing from one side of something to the other.
school: (India, Canada, US) An institution dedicated to teaching and learning; an educational institution.
high: Physically elevated, extending above a base or average level:
each: (operations) An individual item: the least quantitative unit in a grouping.
links: Some text or a graphic in an electronic document that can be activated to display another document or trigger an action.
review: A second or subsequent reading of a text or artifact in an attempt to gain new insights.
years: A solar year, the time it takes the Earth to complete one revolution of the Sun (between 365.24 and 365.26 days depending on the point of reference).
book: A collection of sheets of paper bound together to hinge at one edge, containing printed or written material, pictures, etc.
read: A reading or an act of reading, especially an actor's part of a play.
group: A number of things or persons being in some relation to one another.
company: A team; a group of people who work together professionally.
many: A multitude; a great aggregate; a mass of people; the generality; the common herd.
need: A requirement for something; something needed.
user: One who uses or makes use of something, a consumer/client or an express or implied licensee (free user) or a trespasser.
general: A general fact or proposition; a generality.
research: Diligent inquiry or examination to seek or revise facts, principles, theories, applications, etc.; laborious or continued search after truth.
full: Containing the maximum possible amount that can fit in the space available.
university: Institution of higher education (typically accepting students from the age of about 17 or 18, depending on country, but in some exceptional cases able to take younger students) where subjects are studied and researched in depth and degrees are offered.
map: A visual representation of an area, whether real or imaginary.
reviews: A second or subsequent reading of a text or artifact in an attempt to gain new insights.
life: The state of organisms preceding their death, characterized by biological processes such as metabolism and reproduction and distinguishing them from inanimate objects; the state of being alive and living.
management: (uncountable) Administration; the use of limited resources combined with forecasting, planning, leadership and execution skills to achieve predetermined specific goals.
way: (heading) To do with a place or places.
hotel: An establishment that provides accommodation and other services for paying guests; normally larger than a guesthouse, and often one of a chain.
real: True, genuine, not merely nominal or apparent.
center: The point in the interior of a circle that is equidistant from all points on the circumference.
international: Of or having to do with more than one nation.
travel: (intransitive) To be on a journey, often for pleasure or business and with luggage; to go from one place to another.
store: (mainly North American) A building (or portion thereof) where items may be purchased.
must: To do as a requirement; indicates that the sentence subject is required as an imperative or directive to execute the sentence predicate, with failure to do so resulting in a failure or negative consequence.
made: (UK dialectal or obsolete) A grub or maggot.
development: (uncountable) The process of developing; growth, directed change.
member: One who belongs to a group.
report: (transitive, intransitive) To relate details of (an event or incident); to recount, describe (something).
off: In a direction away from the speaker or other reference point.
line: A path through two or more points (compare ‘segment’); a continuous mark, including as made by a pen; any path, curved or straight.
terms: In legal terminology, Terms can have different meanings, depending on the specific context.
hotels: HOTELS is a trade publication serving the information needs of the worldwide hospitality industry.
did: Initialism of direct inward dialing. [The ability to make an external telephone call to an internal extension within an organization.]
type: A grouping based on shared characteristics; a class.
send: (transitive, ditransitive) To make something (such as an object or message) go from one place to another (or to someone).
right: Complying with justice, correctness, or reason; correct, just, true. See also the interjection senses below.
those: (degree) To a given extent or degree.
local: From or in a nearby location.
office: (religion) A ceremonial duty or service, particularly:

national: Pertaining to a nation or country, especially as a whole; affecting, shared by, or existing throughout all of a nation.

education: (uncountable) The process of imparting knowledge, skill and judgment.
take: (transitive) To get into one's hands, possession, or control, with or without force.
design: A plan (with more or less detail) for the structure and functions of an artifact, building or system.
community: (countable) A group sharing common characteristics, such as the same language, law, religion, or tradition.
internet: (uncountable) Internet access or connection; internet connectivity.
within: In the context of which the present document or ruling is made.
area: A measure of the extent of a surface; it is measured in square units.
states: A condition; a set of circumstances applying at any given time.

subject: Likely to be affected by or to experience something.
between: A kind of needle, shorter than a sharp, with a small rounded eye, used for making fine stitches on heavy fabrics.

based: To give as its foundation or starting point; to lay the foundation of.
show: A play, dance, or other entertainment.
code: A short symbol, often with little relation to the item it represents.
special: A reduction in consumer cost (usually for a limited time) for items or services rendered.
check: A situation in which the king is directly threatened by an opposing piece.
prices: The cost required to gain possession of something.
index: An alphabetical listing of items and their location.

sign: (sometimes also used uncountably) A visible indication.
file: A collection of papers collated and archived together.
link: Some text or a graphic in an electronic document that can be activated to display another document or trigger an action.
south: One of the four major compass points, specifically 180°, directed toward the South Pole, and conventionally downwards on a map, abbreviated as S.
technology: The organization of knowledge for practical purposes.
same: Not different or other; not another or others; not different as regards self; selfsame; identical.
case: An actual event, situation, or fact.
project: A planned endeavor, usually with a specific goal and accomplished in several steps or stages.
section: A cutting; a part cut out from the rest of something.
version: A specific form or variation of something.
own: Belonging to; possessed; acquired; proper to; property of; titled to; held in one's name; under/using the name of. Often marks a possessive determiner as reflexive, referring back to the subject of the clause or sentence.
sports: Synonym of sport (“the class of physical activies; athletics”).
house: A structure built or serving as an abode of human beings.
security: (uncountable) The condition of not being threatened, especially physically, psychologically, emotionally, or financially.
american: A citizen or national of the United States of America.
county: An administrative or geographical region of various countries, including Bhutan, Canada, China, Croatia, Ireland, Japan, New Zealand, Norway, Poland, Romania, South Korea, Sweden, the United Kingdom, and 47 of the 50 United States (excluding Alaska, Connecticut, and Louisiana).
members: One who belongs to a group.
power: The ability to do or undergo something.
while: An uncertain duration of time, a period of time.
down: (comparable) From a higher position to a lower one; downwards.
computer: A programmable electronic device that performs mathematical calculations and logical operations, especially one that can process, store and retrieve large amounts of data very quickly; now especially, a small one for personal or home use employed for manipulating text or graphics, accessing the Internet, or playing games or media.

total: An amount obtained by the addition of smaller amounts.
following: Coming next, either in sequence or in time.
end: The terminal point of something in space or time.
place: (physical) An area; somewhere within an area.
download: (transitive, computing) To transfer data to a given computer from a remote one via a network.
access: (uncountable) The right or ability of approaching or entering; admittance; admission; accessibility.
per: (rare, nonstandard) Belonging to per, their (singular). Gender-neutral third-person singular possessive adjective, coordinate with gendered his and her.
think: (transitive) To ponder, to go over in one's mind.
current: Existing or occurring at the moment.
north: The direction towards the pole to the left-hand side of someone facing east, specifically 0°, or (on another celestial object) the direction towards the pole lying on the northern side of the invariable plane.
resources: Something that one uses to achieve an objective, e.g. raw materials or personnel.
media: (often treated as uncountable) The means and institutions for publishing and broadcasting information.
posts: A long dowel or plank protruding from the ground; a fencepost; a lightpost.
water: A barangay of Baco, Oriental Mindoro, Philippines (unconfirmed).
control: (transitive) To exercise influence over; to suggest or dictate the behavior of.

pictures: A representation of anything (as a person, a landscape, a building) upon canvas, paper, or other surface, by drawing, painting, printing, photography, etc.
since: From a specified time in the past.
art: The conscious production or arrangement of sounds, colours, forms, movements, or other elements in a manner that affects the senses and emotions, usually specifically the production of the beautiful in a graphic or plastic medium.
personal: An advertisement by which individuals attempt to meet others with similar interests.
including: To bring into a group, class, set, or total as a (new) part or member.
shop: An establishment that sells goods or services to the public; originally only a physical location, but now a virtual establishment as well.
guide: Someone who guides, especially someone hired to show people around a place or an institution and offer information and explanation.
board: A relatively long, wide and thin piece of any material, usually wood or similar, often for use in construction or furniture-making.
location: A particular point or place in physical space.

text: A writing consisting of multiple glyphs, characters, symbols or sentences.
white: The color/colour of snow or milk; the colour of light containing equal amounts of all visible wavelengths.
small: Any part of something that is smaller or slimmer than the rest, now usually with anatomical reference to the back.
rating: To assign or be assigned a particular rank or level.
government: The body with the power to make and/or enforce laws to control a country, land area, people or organization.
children: A person who has not yet reached adulthood, whether natural (puberty), cultural (initiation), or legal (majority)

old: Of an object, concept, relationship, etc., having existed for a relatively long period of time.
love: (uncountable) A deep caring for the existence of another.
image: A visual or other representation of the external form of something in art.
call: (heading) To reach out with one's voice.

why: (interrogative) For what cause, reason, or purpose.

listing: An entry in a list or directory.

low: Situated close to, or even below, the ground or another normal reference plane; not high or lofty.

customer: A patron, a client; a person or company who purchases or receives a product or service from a business or merchant, or intends to do so.
compare: (transitive) To assess the similarities and differences between two or more things ["to compare X with Y"]. Having made the comparison of X with Y, one might have found it similar to Y or different from Y.
college: (chiefly US) An institution of higher education teaching undergraduates.
value: The quality that renders something desirable or valuable; worth.
include: To bring into a group, class, set, or total as a (new) part or member.
article: A piece of nonfiction writing such as a story, report, opinion piece, or entry in a newspaper, magazine, journal, encyclopedia, etc.
food: (uncountable) Any solid substance that can be consumed by living organisms, especially by eating, in order to sustain life.
provide: To give what is needed or desired, especially basic needs.
different: Not the same; exhibiting a difference.

around: (with the verb "to be") Present in the vicinity.

join: An intersection of piping or wiring; an interconnect.
credit: Reliance on the truth of something said or done; faith; trust.
science: A particular discipline or branch of learning, especially one dealing with measurable or systematic principles rather than intuition or natural ability.
categories: A group, often named or numbered, to which items are assigned based on similarity or defined criteria.
west: One of the four principal compass points, specifically 270°, conventionally directed to the left on maps; the direction of the setting sun at an equinox, abbreviated as W.
english: Spinning or rotary motion given to a ball around the vertical axis, as in billiards or bowling.
look: To try to see, to pay attention to with one’s eyes.
team: A set of draught animals, such as two horses in front of a carriage.
box: Senses relating to a three-dimensional object or space.
estate: The collective property and liabilities of someone, especially a deceased person.

live: To be alive; to have life.
category: A group, often named or numbered, to which items are assigned based on similarity or defined criteria.
table: (countable) Furniture with a top surface to accommodate a variety of uses.

large: Of considerable or relatively great size or extent.

yes: An affirmative expression; an answer that shows agreement or acceptance.
second: Number-two; following after the first one with nothing between them. The ordinal number corresponding to the cardinal number two.

going: (in combination) Attending or visiting (a stated event, place, etc.) habitually or regularly.
questions: A game in which players must only say questions, and if they don't they lose.
medical: Of or pertaining to the practice of medicine.
friend: A person, typically someone other than a family member, spouse or lover, whose company one enjoys and towards whom one feels affection.
study: (usually academic, transitive, intransitive) To review materials already learned in order to make sure one does not forget them, usually in preparation for an examination.

looking: The act of one who looks; a glance.
issues: the fourth studio album by American nu metal band Korn.

users: One who uses or makes use of something, a consumer or client or an express or implied licensee (free user) or a trespasser.
never: At no time; on no occasion; in no circumstance.

street: A paved part of road, usually in a village or a town.

tax: Money paid to the government other than for transaction-specific goods and services.
standard: A principle or example or measure used for comparison.
person: An individual; usually a human being.
mobile: A kinetic sculpture or decorative arrangement made of items hanging so that they can move independently from each other.
blog: A website that allows users to reflect, share opinions, and discuss various topics in the form of an online journal, sometimes letting readers comment on their posts. Most blogs are written in a slightly informal tone (personal journals, news, businesses, etc.)
equipment: The act of equipping, or the state of being equipped, as for a voyage or expedition.
party: A person or group of people constituting a particular side in a contract or legal action.

stores: A place where items may be accumulated or routinely kept.
recent: Having happened a short while ago.
problem: A difficulty that has to be resolved or dealt with.
side: A bounding straight edge of a two-dimensional shape.
give: (ditransitive) To move, shift, provide something abstract or concrete to someone or something or somewhere.
memory: (uncountable) The ability of the brain to record information or impressions with the facility of recalling them later, usually at will.

story: An account of real or fictional events.

sell: (transitive, ditransitive, intransitive) To transfer goods or provide services in exchange for money.
experience: (usually uncountable) A collection of events and/or activities from which an individual or group may gather knowledge, opinions, and skills.
rates: (British, New Zealand, Australia, Hong Kong) Taxes, usually on property, levied by local government.
options: a 1975 absurdist science fiction novel by American writer Robert Sheckley, published in paperback by Pyramid Books.
key: (countable) An object designed to open and close a lock.
create: (transitive) To bring into existence; (sometimes in particular:)
field: A land area free of woodland, cities, and towns; an area of open country.
important: Having relevant and crucial value; having import.
young: In the early part of growth or life; born not long ago.
paper: A sheet material typically used for writing on or printing on (or as a non-waterproof container), usually made by draining cellulose fibres from a suspension in water.
east: The direction of the earth's rotation, specifically 90°.
activities: (countable) Something done as an action or a movement.
age: (countable) The amount of time that some being has been alive, or that some thing has been in existence, as measured from its birth or origin until the present or until some other given reference point. (Often measured in number of years; alternatively in months, days, hours, etc.; see also the usage notes)
single: Not accompanied by anything else; one in number.
club: (countable) A heavy object, often a kind of stick, intended for use as a bludgeoning weapon or a plaything.
example: Something that is representative of all such things in a group.
road: A way used for travelling between places, originally one wide enough to allow foot passengers and horses to travel, now (US) usually one surfaced with asphalt or concrete and designed to accommodate many vehicles travelling in both directions. In the UK both senses are heard: a country road is the same as a country lane.
something: Having a characteristic that the speaker cannot specify.
gift: Something given to another voluntarily, without charge.
question: A worded or expressed sentence, phrase, or only a word on its own, which asks for information, a reply, or a response; an interrogative.
changes: (music) Ellipsis of chord changes. [(more specifically) A chord progression.]
hard: (of material or fluid) Solid and firm.
night: (countable) The time when the Sun is below the horizon when the sky is dark.
pay: (ambitransitive) To give money or other compensation to in exchange for goods or services.
browse: To scan, to casually look through in order to find items of interest, especially without knowledge of what to look for beforehand.
status: A person’s condition, position or standing relative to that of others.
four: (countable) The digit or figure 4; an occurrence thereof.
range: A line or series of mountains, buildings, etc.
issue: The action or an instance of flowing or coming out, an outflow, particularly:
building: (countable) A closed structure with walls and a roof.
court: An enclosed space; a courtyard; an uncovered area shut in by the walls of a building, or by different buildings; also, a space opening from a street and nearly surrounded by houses; a blind alley.
always: At all times; throughout all time; since the beginning.
seller: Someone who sells; a vendor; a clerk.
light: Visible electromagnetic radiation. The human eye can typically detect radiation (light) in the wavelength range of about 400 to 750 nanometers. Nearby shorter and longer wavelength ranges, although not visible, are commonly called ultraviolet and infrared light.
audio: A sound, or a sound signal
result: That which results; the conclusion or end to which any course or condition of things leads, or which is obtained by any process or operation; consequence or effect.
war: Organized, large-scale, armed conflict between countries or between national, ethnic, or other sizeable groups, usually involving the engagement of military forces.
write: The act or style of writing.
offer: A proposal that has been made.
blue: The colour of the clear sky or the deep sea, between green and violet in the visible spectrum, and one of the primary additive colours for transmitted light; the colour obtained by subtracting red and green from white light using magenta and cyan filters; or any colour resembling this.
groups: A number of things or persons being in some relation to one another.
files: A collection of papers collated and archived together.
given: (ditransitive) To move, shift, provide something abstract or concrete to someone or something or somewhere.
request: Act of requesting (with the adposition at in the presence of possessives, and on in their absence).
analysis: Decomposition into components in order to study (a complex thing, concept, theory etc.).
release: The event of setting (someone or something) free (e.g. hostages, slaves, prisoners, caged animals, hooked or stuck mechanisms).
making: The act of forming, causing, or constituting; workmanship; construction.
picture: A representation of anything (as a person, a landscape, a building) upon canvas, paper, or other surface, by drawing, painting, printing, photography, etc.
needs: Of necessity; necessarily; indispensably; used with an auxiliary verb (often must), and equivalent to "of need".
might: Power, strength, force or influence held by a person or group.
professional: A person who belongs to a profession
yet: (usually with negative) Thus far; up to the present; up to some specified time; still
month: A period into which a year is divided, historically based on the phases of the moon.
areas: A measure of the extent of a surface; it is measured in square units.
major: A military rank between captain and lieutenant colonel.
star: Any small luminous dot appearing in the cloudless portion of the night sky, especially with a fixed location relative to other such dots.
committee: A body of one or more persons convened for the accomplishment of some specific purpose, typically with formal protocols.
future: The time ahead; those moments yet to be experienced.
sun: A star, especially when seen as the centre of any single solar system.
hand: The part of the forelimb below the forearm or wrist in a human, and the corresponding part in many other animals.
interest: (uncountable) A great attention and concern from someone or something; intellectual curiosity.
become: (copulative, rather formal, followed by an adjective or a noun) begin to be; turn into (often with permanent states).
problems: A difficulty that has to be resolved or dealt with.
keep: (transitive) To continue in (a course or mode of action); to not intermit or fall from; to uphold or maintain.
enter: (intransitive) To go or come into an enclosed or partially enclosed space.
child: (broadly) A person who has not yet reached adulthood, whether natural (puberty), cultural (initiation), or legal (majority).
garden: An outdoor area containing one or more types of plants, usually plants grown for food or ornamental purposes.
share: To give part of what one has to somebody else to use or consume.
similar: Having traits or characteristics in common; alike, allied, comparable.
million: (in roman numerals, m written with a macron over it) denoting a quantity consisting of 1,000,000 items or units
schools: (India, Canada, US) An institution dedicated to teaching and learning; an educational institution.
baby: A very young human, particularly from birth to a couple of years old or until walking is fully mastered.
learning: (uncountable) An act in which something is learned.
popular: Liked by many people; generally pleasing, widely admired.
term: That which limits the extent of anything; limit, extremity, bound, boundary, terminus.
stories: an American early 1970s rock and pop music band based in New York City.
film: (uncountable) A visual art form that consists of a sequence of still images preserved on a recording medium to give the illusion of motion; movies generally.
put: To physically place (something or someone somewhere).
reports: (transitive, intransitive) To relate details of (an event or incident); to recount, describe (something).
journal: A diary or daily record of a person, organization, vessel etc.; daybook.
computers: A programmable electronic device that performs mathematical calculations and logical operations, especially one that can process, store and retrieve large amounts of data very quickly; now especially, a small one for personal or home use employed for manipulating text or graphics, accessing the Internet, or playing games or media.
welcome: The act of greeting someone’s arrival, especially by saying "Welcome!"; reception.
central: Being very important, or key to something.
try: To attempt; to endeavour. Followed by infinitive.
notice: (chiefly uncountable) The act of observing; perception.
president: The head of state of a republic.
images: artifacts that depict visual perception, such as photographs or other two-dimensional pictures.
radio: (countable) A device that can capture (receive) the signal sent over radio waves and render the modulated signal as sound.
head: (countable) The part of the body of an animal or human which contains the brain, mouth, and main sense organs.
self: One individual's personality, character, demeanor, or disposition.
color: (uncountable) The spectral composition of visible light.
cell: (biology) The basic unit of a living organism, consisting of a quantity of protoplasm surrounded by a cell membrane, which is able to synthesize proteins and replicate itself.
includes: One who or that which is eligible to be included.
council: A committee that leads or governs (e.g. city council, student council).
discussion: Conversation or debate concerning a particular topic.
track: A mark left by something that has passed along.
australia: A country consisting of a main island, the island of Tasmania and other smaller islands, located in Oceania; historically, a collection of former colonies of the British Empire. Official name: Commonwealth of Australia. Capital: Canberra.
others: An other, another (person, etc), more often rendered as another.
archive: A place for storing earlier, and often historical, material. An archive usually contains documents (letters, records, newspapers, etc.) or other types of media kept for historical interest.
agreement: An understanding between entities to follow a specific course of conduct.
format: The layout of a publication or document.
entertainment: An activity designed to give pleasure, enjoyment, diversion, amusement, or relaxation to an audience, no matter whether the audience participates passively as in watching opera or a movie, or actively as in games.
least: Something of the smallest possible extent; an indivisible unit.
months: A period into which a year is divided, historically based on the phases of the moon.
society: A long-standing group of people sharing cultural aspects such as language, dress, norms of behavior and artistic forms.
sure: Physically secure and certain, non-failing, reliable.
friends: A person other than a family member, spouse or lover whose company one enjoys and towards whom one feels affection.
safety: The condition or feeling of being safe; security; certainty.
trade: Buying and selling of goods and services on a market.
edition: A written work edited and published, as by a certain editor or in a certain manner.
cars: A wheeled vehicle that moves independently, with at least three wheels, powered mechanically, steered by a driver and mostly for personal transportation.
messages: A communication, or what is communicated; any concept or information conveyed.
tell: A reflexive, often habitual behavior, especially one occurring in a context that often features attempts at deception by persons under psychological stress (such as a poker game or police interrogation), that reveals information that the person exhibiting the behavior is attempting to withhold.
marketing: To make (products or services) available for sale and promote them.
updated: To bring (a thing) up to date.
already: Prior to some specified time, either past, present, or future; by this time; previously.
studies: An academic field of study concerning the given subject.
drive: Motivation to do or achieve something; ability coupled with ambition.
common: Mutual good, shared by more than one.
gold: (uncountable) A heavy yellow elemental metal of great value, with atomic number 79 and symbol Au.
collection: A set of items or amount of material procured, gathered or presented together.
lot: A large quantity or number; a great deal.
short: Having a small distance from one end or edge to another, either horizontally or vertically.
display: (computing) An electronic screen that shows graphics or text.
limited: With certain (often specified) limits placed upon it.
solutions: An act, plan or other means, used or proposed, to solve a problem.
powered: (of a device) Having its own source of power, or a named source.
means: An instrument or condition to achieve a result.
beach: The shore of a body of water, especially when sandy or pebbly.
director: One who directs; the person in charge of managing a department or directorate (e.g., director of engineering), project, or production (as in a show or film, e.g., film director).
daily: That occurs every day, or at least every working day.
whether: (obsolete, dialect) The retention of the afterbirth in cows or sheep.
past: The period of time that has already happened, in contrast to the present and the future.
electronics: (in the plural) Devices which require the flow of electrons through conductors and semiconductors in order to perform their function; devices that operate on electrical power (battery or outlet)
upon: Used to indicate position on or motion to a surface.
period: (now chiefly Canada, US, Philippines) The punctuation mark “.” (indicating the ending of a sentence or marking an abbreviation).
planning: The act of formulating of a course of action, or of drawing up plans.
database: (computing) A set of tables and other objects (queries, reports, forms) in the form of a structured data set.
average: (statistics) Any measure of central tendency, especially any mean, the median, or the mode.
weather: The short term state of the atmosphere at a specific time and place, including the temperature, relative humidity, cloud cover, precipitation, wind, etc.
land: The part of Earth which is not covered by oceans or other bodies of water.
done: Having completed or finished an activity.
window: An opening, usually covered by one or more panes of clear glass, to allow light and air from outside to enter a building or vehicle.
region: Any considerable and connected part of a space or surface; specifically, a tract of land or sea of considerable but indefinite extent; a country; a district; in a broad sense, a place without special reference to location or extent but viewed as an entity for geographical, social or cultural reasons.
france: A country located primarily in Western Europe. Official name: French Republic. Capital and largest city: Paris.
record: An item of information put into a temporary or permanent physical medium.
island: A contiguous area of land, smaller than a continent, totally surrounded by water.
records: An item of information put into a temporary or permanent physical medium.
conference: (sciences) A formal event where scientists present their research results in speeches, workshops, posters or by other means.
district: An administrative division of an area.
calendar: Any system by which time is divided into days, weeks, months, and years.
costs: To incur a charge of; to require payment of a (specified) price.
front: The foremost side of something or the end that faces the direction it normally moves.
style: Senses relating to a thin, pointed object.
update: An advisement providing more up-to-date information than currently known.
ever: Occurring at any time, occurring even but once during a timespan.
early: A shift (scheduled work period) that takes place early in the day.
downloads: A file transfer to the local computer.
miles: The international mile: a unit of length precisely equal to 1.609344 kilometers established by treaty among Anglophone nations in 1959, divided into 5,280 feet or 1,760 yards.
drug: A substance used to treat an illness, relieve a symptom, or modify a chemical process in the body for a specific purpose.
examples: Something that is representative of all such things in a group.
currently: At this moment, at present, now.
facility: The fact of being easy, or easily done; absence of difficulty, simplicity.
evening: The time of the day between dusk and night, when it gets dark.
particular: A small individual part of something larger; a detail, a point.
clear: Full extent; distance between extreme limits; especially; the distance between the nearest surfaces of two bodies, or the space between walls.
accept: To receive, especially with a consent, with favour, or with approval.
numbers: An abstract entity used to describe quantity.
achieve: To succeed in something, now especially in academic performance.
across: (crosswords, often in combination) A word that runs horizontally in the completed puzzle grid or its associated clue.
accident: An unexpected event with negative consequences occurring without the intention of the one suffering the consequences.
active: A person or thing that is acting or capable of acting.
administration: The act of administering; government of public affairs; the service rendered, or duties assumed, in conducting affairs; the conducting of any office or employment; direction.
adult: A person who has reached the legal age of majority.
admit: (transitive) To allow to enter; to grant entrance (to), whether into a place, into the mind, or into consideration
agency: An establishment engaged in doing business for another; also, the place of business or the district of such an agency.
afraid: Impressed with fear or apprehension; in fear.
afternoon: The part of the day from noon or lunchtime until sunset, evening, or suppertime or 6pm.
ago: (archaic or dialectal) Gone; gone by; gone away; passed; passed away.
agree: (intransitive) To be in harmony about an opinion, statement, or action; to have a consistent idea between two or more people.
agent: One who exerts power, or has the power to act.
ahead: At or towards the front; in the direction one is facing or moving.
airplane: (chiefly US, Canada, Philippines) A powered heavier-than-air aircraft with fixed wings.
airport: An airfield (an area designated for the takeoff and landing of aircraft), including one or more runways and (for commercial airports) one or more passenger terminals.
allow: (transitive, catenative) To permit, to give permission to.
almost: Very close to, but not quite.
among: In the midst of; surrounded by.
animal: (sciences) Any member of the kingdom Animalia of multicellular organisms that are usually mobile, whose cells are not encased in a rigid cell wall (distinguishing them from plants and fungi) and which derive energy solely from the consumption of other organisms (further distinguishing them from plants).
amount: The total, aggregate or sum of material (not applicable to discrete numbers or units or items in standard English).
anymore: (especially US) Alternative form of any more. [(in negative or interrogative constructions) From a given time onwards; longer, again.]
answer: A response or reply; something said or done in reaction to a statement or question.
apparent: Clear or manifest to the understanding; plain; evident; obvious; known; palpable; indubitable.
apartment: (chiefly Canada, US, Australia, Philippines) A complete domicile occupying only part of a building, especially one for rent; a flat.
apart: Placed separately (in regard to space or time).
appear: (intransitive) To come or be in sight; to be in view; to become visible.
appearance: The way something looks; personal presence
apply: (transitive) To put to use; to use or employ for a particular purpose, or in a particular case
appoint: (transitive) To name (someone to a post or role).
apple: A common, firm, round fruit produced by a tree of the genus Malus.
appointment: An arrangement between people to meet; an engagement.
approach: (intransitive) To come or go near, in place or time; to move toward; to advance nearer; to draw nigh.
approve: (transitive) To regard as good; to commend; to be pleased with; to think well of.
approval: An expression granting permission; an indication of agreement with a proposal; an acknowledgement that a person, thing, or event meets requirements.
architect: A professional who designs buildings or other structures, or who prepares plans and superintends construction.
argue: (intransitive) To debate, disagree, or discuss opposing or differing viewpoints; to controvert; to wrangle.
approximately: Imprecise but close to in quantity or amount.
arm: (anatomy) The portion of the upper human appendage, from the shoulder to the wrist and sometimes including the hand.
arise: (intransitive) To come up from one's bed or place of repose; to get up.
argument: (countable, also figuratively) A fact or statement used to support a proposition; a reason.
army: A large, highly organized military force, concerned mainly with ground (rather than air or naval) operations.
armed: (sometimes in combination) Equipped, especially with a weapon.
arrive: To reach; to get to a certain place.
arrival: The act of arriving or something that has arrived.
arrest: A check, stop, an act or instance of arresting something.
ashamed: To feel shame; to be ashamed.
aside: An incidental remark made quietly so as to be heard by the person to whom it is said and not by any others in the vicinity.
assessment: The act of assessing or an amount (of tax, levy or duty etc) assessed.
asleep: In a state of sleep; also, broadly, resting.
aspect: Any specific feature, part, or element of something.
assist: A helpful action or an act of giving.
assistance: Aid; help; the act or result of assisting.
assignment: The act of assigning; the allocation of a job or a set of tasks.
assume: To authenticate by means of belief; to surmise; to suppose to be true, especially without proof
associate: A person united with another or others in an act, enterprise, or business; a partner.
assistant: Someone who is present; a bystander, a witness.
atmosphere: The gases surrounding the Earth or any astronomical body.
assumption: The act of assuming, or taking to or upon oneself; the act of taking up or adopting.
attach: To fasten, to join to (literally and figuratively).
attempt: The action of trying at something.
attack: An attempt to cause damage, injury to, or death of opponent or enemy.
attend: To listen to (something or someone); to pay attention to; regard; heed.
attitude: The position of the body or way of carrying oneself.
attractive: Causing attraction; having the quality of attracting by inherent force.
attorney: A lawyer; one who advises or represents others in legal matters as a profession.
authority: The power to enforce rules or give orders.
audience: A group of people within hearing; specifically, a large gathering of people listening to or watching a performance, speech, etc.
award: A trophy or medal; something that denotes an accomplishment, especially in a competition. A prize or honor based on merit.
background: A part of the picture that depicts scenery to the rear or behind the main subject; context.
avoid: (transitive) To keep away from; to keep clear of; to stay away from.
bag: A soft container made out of cloth, paper, thin plastic, etc. and open at the top, used to hold food, commodities, and other goods.
balance: (uncountable) A state in which opposing forces harmonise; equilibrium.
bake: (transitive or intransitive or ditransitive, with person as subject) To cook (something) in an oven (for someone).
ball: A solid or hollow sphere, or roughly spherical mass.
bank: (countable) An institution where one can place and borrow money and take care of financial affairs.
ban: (transitive) To prohibit; to interdict; to proscribe; to forbid or block from participation.
band: A strip of material used for strengthening or coupling.
base: Something from which other things extend; a foundation.
bar: A solid, more or less rigid object of metal or wood with a uniform cross-section smaller than its length.
basketball: (sports, uncountable) A sport in which two opposing teams of five players strive to put a ball through a hoop.
battery: (electricity, electronics, countable) A device used to power electric devices, consisting of one or more electrically connected electrochemical cells or (archaically) electrostatic cells.
battle: (military) A general action, fight, or encounter, in which all the divisions of an army are or may be engaged; a combat, an engagement.
bathroom: A room containing a shower or bathtub, and (typically but not necessarily) a toilet.
beautiful: Possessing beauty, impressing the eye; attractive.
bear: A large, generally omnivorous mammal (a few species are purely carnivorous or herbivorous), having shaggy fur, a very small tail, and flat feet; a member of the family Ursidae.
bedroom: A room in a house, apartment, hotel, or other dwelling where a bed is kept for sleeping.
beauty: (uncountable) The quality of being (especially visually) attractive, pleasing, fine or good-looking; comeliness.
beer: (uncountable) An alcoholic drink fermented from starch material, commonly barley malt; often with hops or some other substance (like gruit) to impart a bitter flavor.
behind: At or in the rear or back part of something.
begin: (ergative) To start, to initiate or take the first step into something.
behavior: (countable, uncountable) The way or manner a living creature behaves or acts generally.
bell: (music) A percussive instrument made of metal or other hard material, typically but not always in the shape of an inverted cup with a flared rim, which resonates when struck.
believe: (transitive) To accept as true, particularly without absolute certainty (i.e., as opposed to knowing).
belief: Mental acceptance of a claim as true.
bench: A long seat with or without a back, found for example in parks and schools.
belt: (clothing) A band worn around the waist to hold clothing to one's body (usually pants), hold weapons (such as a gun or sword), or serve as a decorative piece of clothing.
bend: (transitive) To cause (something) to change its shape into a curve, by physical force, chemical action, or any other means.
benefit: An advantage; help or aid from something.
bicycle: A vehicle that has two primary wheels, one behind the other, a steering handle, and a saddle seat or seats and is usually propelled by the action of a rider’s feet upon pedals.
bill: (US, Canada) A piece of paper money; a banknote.
bike: Any vehicle sharing some characteristics with a bicycle or motorbike, such as pedal power, a handlebar, or a saddle.
bind: (intransitive) To tie; to confine by any ligature.
bird: An animal of the clade (traditionally class) Aves in the phylum Chordata, characterized by being warm-blooded, having feathers and wings usually capable of flight, having a beaked mouth, and laying eggs.
billion: a name for a large number.
birthday: The anniversary of the day on which someone is born.
bite: (transitive) To cut into something by clamping the teeth.
birth: (uncountable) The process of childbearing; the beginning of life; the emergence of a human baby or other viviparous animal offspring from the mother's body into the environment.
black: (of an object) Absorbing all light and reflecting none; dark and hueless.
blame: (transitive, usually followed by "for") To assert or consider that someone is the cause of something negative; to place blame; to attribute responsibility (for something negative or for doing something negative).
bit: (mathematics, computing) A binary digit, generally represented as a 1 or 0.
block: A substantial, often approximately cuboid, piece of any substance.
blood: A member of the Los Angeles gang The Bloods.
blind: A covering for a window to keep out light. The covering may be made of cloth or of narrow slats that can block light or allow it to pass.
boil: A localized accumulation of pus in the skin, resulting from infection.
boat: A craft used for transportation of goods, fishing, racing, recreational cruising, or military use on or in the water, propelled by oars or outboard motor or inboard motor or by wind.
bone: A composite material consisting largely of calcium phosphate and collagen and making up the skeleton of most vertebrates.
bond: Evidence of a long-term debt, by which the bond issuer (the borrower) is obliged to pay interest when due, and repay the principal at maturity, as specified on the face of the bond certificate. The rights of the holder are specified in the bond indenture, which contains the legal terms and conditions under which the bond was issued. Bonds are available in two forms: registered bonds, and bearer bonds.
bomb: An explosive device used or intended as a weapon.
boot: A heavy shoe that covers part of the leg.
bonus: Something extra that is good; an added benefit.
boom: A low-pitched, resonant sound, such as of an explosion.
born: To carry or convey, literally or figuratively.
borrow: Deviation of the path of a rolling ball from a straight line; slope; slant.
bottle: A container, typically made of glass or plastic and having a tapered neck, used primarily for holding liquids.
boss: A person who oversees and directs the work of others; a supervisor.
boundary: The dividing line or location between two areas.
bowl: A roughly hemispherical container used to hold, mix or present food, such as salad, fruit or soup, or other items.
brain: The control center of the central nervous system of an animal located in the skull which is responsible for perception, cognition, attention, memory, emotion, and action.
branch: The woody part of a tree arising from the trunk and usually dividing.
breakfast: The first meal of the day, usually eaten in the morning.
break: An instance of breaking something into two or more pieces.
bread: A foodstuff made by baking dough made from cereals.
breathe: (intransitive) To draw air into (inhale), and expel air from (exhale), the lungs in order to extract oxygen and excrete waste gases.
brick: (countable) A hardened rectangular block of mud, clay etc., used for building.
breath: (uncountable) The act or process of breathing.
bright: Emitting much light; visually dazzling; luminous, lucent, radiant.
bridge: A construction or natural feature that spans a divide.
brown: (countable and uncountable) A colour like that of chocolate or coffee.
brush: An implement consisting of multiple more or less flexible bristles or other filaments attached to a handle, used for any of various purposes including cleaning, painting, and arranging hair.
brother: Son of the same parents as another person.
bullet: A projectile, usually of metal, shot from a gun at high speed.
build: (transitive) To form (something) by combining materials or parts.
budget: The amount of money or resources earmarked for a particular institution, activity or timeframe.
burn: A physical injury caused by heat, cold, electricity, radiation or caustic chemicals.
bunch: A group of similar things, either growing together, or in a cluster or clump, usually fastened together.
bus: (automotive) A motor vehicle for transporting large numbers of people along roads.
bury: To ritualistically inter in a grave or tomb.
button: (clothing) A knob or disc that is passed through a loop or (buttonhole), serving as a fastener.
butter: A soft, fatty foodstuff made by churning the cream of milk (generally cow's milk).
busy: Crowded with business or activities; having a great deal going on.
cabin: The passenger area of an airplane.
cabinet: A group of advisors to a government or business entity.
buyer: A person who makes one or more purchases.
cake: A rich, sweet dessert food, typically made of flour, sugar, and eggs and baked in an oven, and often covered in icing.
cable: (material) A long object used to make a physical connection.
calculate: (transitive, mathematics) To determine the value of something or the solution to something by a mathematical process.
camp: An outdoor place acting as temporary accommodation in tents or other simple structures.
calm: (of a person) Peaceful, quiet, especially free from anger and anxiety.
camera: (photography) A device for taking still or moving pictures or photographs.
cancer: (medicine, oncology, pathology) A disease in which the cells of a tissue undergo uncontrolled (and often rapid) proliferation.
campaign: A series of operations undertaken to achieve a set goal.
capacity: The ability to hold, receive, or absorb.
candidate: A person who seeks to be elected or appointed to a position or privilege.
capable: Able and efficient; having the ability needed for a specific task; having the disposition to do something; permitting or being susceptible to something.
capture: (transitive) To take control of; to seize by force or stratagem.
captain: The person lawfully in command of a ship or other vessel.
capital: (uncountable, business, finance, insurance) Money and wealth. The means to acquire goods and services, especially in a non-barter system.
career: One’s calling in life; one's working occupation or profession, especially when pursued seriously and/or over a long period of time.
carbon: (uncountable) The chemical element (symbol C) with an atomic number of 6. It can be found in pure form for example as graphite, a black, shiny and very soft material, or diamond, a colourless, transparent, crystalline solid and the hardest known material.
careful: Taking care; attentive to potential danger, error or harm; cautious.
carpet: A fabric used as a complete floor covering.
carry: A manner of transporting or lifting something; the grip or position in which something is carried.
catch: The act of seizing or capturing.
cash: Money in the form of notes/bills and coins, as opposed to cheques/checks or electronic transactions.
ceiling: To line or finish (a surface, such as a wall), with plaster, stucco, thin boards, or similar.
cause: (often with of, typically of adverse results) The source of, or reason for, an event or action; that which produces or effects a result.
celebrate: To extol or honour in a solemn manner.
century: A period of 100 consecutive years; often specifically a numbered period with conventional start and end dates, e.g., the twentieth century, which stretches from (strictly) 1901 through 2000, or (informally) 1900 through 1999. The first century AD was from 1 to 100.
cemetery: A place where the dead are buried; a graveyard or memorial park.
celebration: The formal performance of a solemn rite, such as Christian sacrament.
ceremony: A ritual, with religious or cultural significance.
certainly: In a way which is certain; with certainty.
chair: An item of furniture used to sit on or in, comprising a seat, legs, back, and sometimes arm rests, for use by one person. Compare stool, couch, sofa, settee, loveseat and bench.
chain: A series of interconnected rings or links usually made of metal.
champion: An ongoing winner in a game or contest.
channel: The physical confine of a river or slough, consisting of a bed and banks.
character: A being involved in the action of a story.
chapter: (authorship) One of the main sections into which the text of a book is divided.
charge: The amount of money levied for a service.
charity: An organization, the objective of which is to carry out a charitable purpose.
chemical: Any specific chemical element or chemical compound or alloy.
cheek: The soft skin on each side of the face, below the eyes; the outer surface of the sides of the oral cavity.
chest: A box, now usually a large strong box with a secure convex lid.
childhood: The time during which one is a child, from between infancy and puberty.
chief: The leader or head of a tribe, organisation, business unit, or other group.
chicken: (countable) A domesticated subspecies of red junglefowl (Gallus gallus domesticus).
chip: A small piece broken from a larger piece of solid material.
choose: To pick; to make the choice of; to select.
choice: An option; a decision; an opportunity to choose or select something.
circle: (geometry) A two-dimensional geometric figure, a line, consisting of the set of all those points in a plane that are equally distant from a given point (center).
cigarette: A small cigar consisting of tobacco or another substance, wrapped up in a thin roll with paper, intended for smoking.
church: (countable, Christianity) A Christian house of worship; a building where Christian religious services take place.
citizen: A legally recognized member of a state, with associated rights and obligations; a person considered in terms of this role.
cite: (transitive) To quote; to repeat, as a passage from a book, or the words of another.
circumstance: Something which is related to, or in some way affects, a fact or event.
classroom: A room, often in a school, where classes take place.
civil: (comparable) Behaving in a reasonable or polite manner; avoiding displays of hostility.
clean: Free of dirt, filth, or impurities (extraneous matter); not dirty, filthy, or soiled.
client: A customer, a buyer or receiver of goods or services.
clinic: A medical facility, such as a hospital, especially one for the treatment and diagnosis of outpatients.
climate: The long-term manifestations of weather and other atmospheric conditions in a given area or country, now usually represented by the statistical summary of its weather conditions during a period long enough to ensure that representative values are obtained (generally 30 years).
climb: (intransitive) To ascend; rise; to go up.
clothing: Any of a wide variety of articles, usually made of fabrics, animal hair, animal skin, or some combination thereof, used to cover the human body for warmth, to preserve modesty, or for fashion.
clock: A chronometer, an instrument that measures time, particularly the time of day.
clue: Information which may lead one to a certain point or conclusion.
coach: (originally Oxford University slang) A trainer or instructor.
cloud: A visible mass of water droplets suspended in the air.
coat: (countable, clothing) An outer garment covering the upper torso and arms.
coal: (uncountable) A black or brownish black rock formed from prehistoric plant remains, composed largely of carbon and burned as a fuel.
coast: The edge of the land where it meets an ocean, sea, gulf, bay, or large lake.
coffee: (uncountable) A beverage made by infusing the beans of the coffee plant in hot water.
cold: (of a thing) Having a low temperature.
collapse: (intransitive) To break apart and fall down suddenly; to cave in.
column: A vertical line of entries in a table, usually read from top to bottom.
colleague: A fellow member of a profession, staff, academic faculty or other organization; an associate, a workmate.
combine: (transitive) To bring (two or more things or activities) together; to unite.
combination: The act of combining, the state of being combined or the result of combining.
comfortable: Providing physical comfort and ease; agreeable.
commitment: Promise or agreement to do something in the future, especially:
commit: (transitive) To do (something bad); to perpetrate, as a crime, sin, or fault.
commission: A fee charged by an agent or broker for carrying out a transaction.
communication: The act or fact of communicating anything; transmission.
compete: To be in battle or in a rivalry with another for the same thing, position, or reward; to contend
competitor: A person or organization against whom one is competing.
complain: To express feelings of pain, dissatisfaction, or resentment.
component: A smaller, self-contained part of a larger entity. Often refers to a manufactured object that is part of a larger device.
complicated: To make complex; to modify so as to make something intricate or difficult.
composition: The act of putting together; assembly.
compose: To make something by merging parts.
concentration: The act, process or ability of concentrating; the process of becoming concentrated, or the state of being concentrated.
concept: An abstract and general idea; an abstraction.
concentrate: A substance that is in a condensed form.
